It teaches the agent to use the native `bifrost` binary directly, run a strong preflight for repo/app/platform context, prefer `git` before `gh`, pick sensible project/service defaults, and finish with deployment URLs and actionable diagnostics.
- implementation-planning: Produce an implementation plan so complete and unambiguous that a low- or medium-effort agent can build it mechanically, without re-deriving decisions. Use this whenever the user is planning, designing, scoping, or brainstorming a coding task and intends to implement it later — especially if they say things like "plan this", "design the approach", "before we code", "lay out the steps", or mention switching to a cheaper/faster model for the build. Trigger even when the user just describes a feature and asks how to approach it. The goal is a hand-off artifact: front-load all the reasoning here so implementation is translation, not invention.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). # auxilio-extension

Google **Chrome extension (MV3)** for Auxilio (Office Ops) — the **desktop** surface for registering office visitors directly inside Google Calendar. It is the richer counterpart to the `auxilio-workspace` Calendar add-on: where the add-on is mobile-capable but lives under the CardService "card ceiling," the extension gives a full React UI (realtime guest updates, per-guest invite toggle, template preview, inline edits, intentional auto-nudge).

> Read the umbrella `../CLAUDE.md` for the four constraints and UX rules — they apply here.
> Strategy + approval analysis: `../Planning-docs/2026-06-18_calendar_visitor_chrome_extension_decision.md`.
> Build plan: the approved plan that scaffolded this repo.

## What this is (and isn't)

- **Is:** an MV3 extension (WXT + React 19 + TS) that injects a trigger into Google Calendar (web) and renders the registration flow in the **side panel**. A **channel surface / dumb adapter** — it forwards intent to `office-ops-engine`.
- **Isn't:** a place for business logic. Dedup, draft curation, invite/pass issuance, and lifecycle all live in `office-ops-engine`'s visitor plugin. This repo renders UI and calls the engine.
- **Isn't:** mobile. Extensions are desktop-Chrome only. The `auxilio-workspace` add-on remains the mobile surface. Both key off the same **magic address**.

## Architecture — three MV3 surfaces

- **`entrypoints/background.ts`** (service worker) — the only holder of tokens + network. Mints/caches the Google `id_token` (engine auth) + `access_token` (Calendar read), runs the Calendar API client and the engine `/addon/*` client, keeps per-tab event context, routes messages.
- **`entrypoints/calendar.content.ts`** (content script @ `calendar.google.com`) — a **dumb trigger/observer**: detect open event, decode `eid`, inject the "Register a visitor" button (Phase 1) and the magic-address banner (Phase 2). No tokens, no business logic.
- **`entrypoints/sidepanel/`** (the app) — React + TanStack Query UI. Pure presentation + server state.

```
content script ──msg──> background ──HTTPS(id_token)──> office-ops-engine /api/visitor/calendar/addon/*
side panel    ──msg──> background ──Calendar API(access_token)──> canonical iCalUID + attendees
```

## Hard rules

1. **Calendar-only scopes. Never add Gmail/Drive.** Calendar is *sensitive* (verification, no paid audit). Gmail/Drive are *restricted* → annual CASA assessment. Hard line, same as the add-on.
2. **Engine owns the contract.** The extension calls the existing `/api/visitor/calendar/addon/{draft,preview,send,cancel-guest}` routes (ID-token authed). Do **not** add engine signature/scope changes from here. The only engine-side change is env: add this extension's OAuth client id to `AUXILIO_WORKSPACE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` (audience allow-list).
3. **Background is the only secret/network holder.** Content script stays a dumb trigger; side panel stays presentation. Keep the thin-adapter boundary.
4. **Send the canonical `iCalUID`** (from the Calendar API `events.get`, not synthesized) so the extension path converges on the same `calendar_event_links` row as `.ics`/OAuth/add-on.
5. **Design: Material 3 tokens, not its code.** Our own `design/tokens.ts` + `global.css` (primary `#92288E`). No MUI/Tailwind/paper. **Icons: lucide-react only**, no Unicode glyphs/emoji in chrome.
6. **Animations subtle, only where needed** — CSS transitions driven by motion tokens; respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
7. **Internal-first.** Force-installed/unlisted to our Workspace → no OAuth verification yet. Public/commercial = a later packaging + verification exercise (CWS review + OAuth verification), per the decision doc.

## Stack & commands

WXT (MV3 over Vite) + React 19 + TypeScript + TanStack Query (server state) + Zustand (UI/session/event-context) + lucide-react.

```bash
npm install
npm run dev        # WXT dev server; load .output/chrome-mv3 in chrome://extensions (Developer mode → Load unpacked)
npm run build      # production build → .output/chrome-mv3
npm run compile    # tsc --noEmit (type-check)
npm run zip        # packaged zip for store upload
```

Env: copy `.env.example` → `.env` (engine URL, OAuth client id, magic address). `WXT_` vars are inlined at build.

## Versioning

content.text:
I've traced the full path that feeds that homescreen list. Here's how it works and where it's most likely breaking for you.

## The mechanism

The "Visitor events needing passes" list is **not** read live from the open event. It comes entirely from a background sync cache:

`useVisitorEvents` → `LIST_VISITOR_EVENTS` → `runSync()` → `listEvents()` → filter by `isMarked()` → stored in `storage.local` → `listMarked()`

So for an event to appear, **all** of these must be true:
1. `runSync` ran with a valid Calendar access token,
2. the event is on the **primary calendar** (`calendar-sync.ts` / `listEvents` only queries `calendars/primary/events`),
3. the magic address is an **attendee or in the location text**, and
4. it matches `MAGIC_ADDRESS` exactly (case-insensitive).

## Most likely causes, ranked

**1. Magic-address mismatch (most likely).** Your `.env` has:
```
WXT_MAGIC_ADDRESS=findajx@gmail.com
```
…but `.env.example` says `visitors@auxilio.app`. `MAGIC_ADDRESS` is **inlined at build time** (`import.meta.env`), so the loaded extension only matches whatever address it was compiled with. If the event you created used a different address than the build's, `isMarked()` returns `false` and the event never enters the cache. → Confirm: what address is actually on the event vs. what build is loaded?

**2. Event is on a secondary calendar (known, documented gap).** `listEvents` is **primary-calendar-only** — there's an explicit comment about this in `lib/calendar.ts:157` and it's in your memory (`project_extension_nudge_reliability` → "secondary calendars deferred, no calendarlist scope"). If the host created the visitor event on a secondary calendar they own, sync never sees it (the in-page nudge would still fire if you *open* it, but the homescreen list won't show it).

**3. Magic address is only the organizer, not an attendee.** The sync list fetch requests `attendees(email,resource)` + `location` only. `isMarked` checks attendees and location — if the magic address is *just* the organizer and not also in the attendee list, it's missed.

**4. Sync didn't run/succeed.** If the access token lapsed, the toolbar badge shows a red `!`. If `runSync` threw, the query errors and the same empty card renders. Quick tell: does the toolbar icon show a count badge?

## Fastest way to pin it

Open the extension's **service-worker console** (`chrome://extensions` → Auxilio → "service worker") and watch the `[auxilio]` logs while the panel loads. Right now `runSync` doesn't log its match count, so the quickest confirmation is a temporary diagnostic line. Want me to add a log in `runSync` that prints `MAGIC_ADDRESS`, total items pulled, and how many matched `isMarked`? That single output will instantly tell us whether it's a mismatch (cause #1/#3) vs. simply-not-seen (cause #2, secondary calendar).

Before I touch code — can you tell me:
- **What email address is on the event** (and is it an attendee or in the location), and
- **Is the event on the host's primary Google Calendar, or a secondary one they own?**

That'll let me go straight to the right fix instead of guessing.

content.text: I've now read the whole flow end to end — content script, background sync, side panel, auth, and the engine RPC contract. The three things you saw are not three unrelated bugs; they share **one root cause**, plus there are a couple of genuine secondary flaws. Here's the full picture. ## The core finding For this event, **the background sync never "saw" it, and the only thing that fired was the content script's DOM‑optimistic fallback — which is a degraded path.** Everything you observed follows from that. Proof it's the optimistic path, not the real sync: - The nudge banner text is built as `"Visitor event needs passes. " + (t.title || '')` (`calendar.content.ts:965`). You got the bold line with **no name** → `title` was empty. - A **synced** target always carries a title (`ev.summary`, `calendar-sync.ts:95`). An **optimistic** target gets its title from the DOM snapshot. So a title‑less nudge proves it came from the optimistic path and the sync **never replaced it** (if sync had found the event, `setTargets` would have swapped in the titled version, `calendar.content.ts:1003‑1011`). And one important thing this rules out: **the magic address is NOT mismatched.** The optimistic nudge only fires when `lastSnapshot.magicPresent` is true, i.e. the page text literally contained your `MAGIC_ADDRESS` (`calendar.content.ts:218`, `:239`). So the build's magic address matches the event. The problem is purely that **sync didn't pick the event up**. ## Why each symptom happened **1. "No upcoming visitor events found" on the homescreen.** That list is 100% sync‑derived: `useVisitorEvents` → `LIST_VISITOR_EVENTS` → `runSync` + `listMarked` (`hooks.ts:179`, `background.ts:295`). It has **no optimistic fallback**. Since sync didn't see the event, the list is empty — even though you were about to look at a real visitor event. **2. Nudge with no event name.** The optimistic title comes from `snapshotOf`, which reads the title **only from an ``**: ```ts const titleInput = el.querySelector('input[aria-label*="title" i], input[placeholder*="title" i]'); const title = titleInput?.value?.trim() || undefined; // calendar.content.ts:223‑227 ``` A title input exists **only on the full edit page**. You opened the event's **detail popover** (clicked the chip), where the title is a heading, not an input → `title = undefined` → nameless nudge. Normally the sync would backfill the real title within ~8s; here sync never confirmed the event, so it stayed nameless for the 12s optimistic TTL and then vanished. **3. "Manage Visitors" button missing the first time.** Two things combine: - You opened the **panel first**, so when you opened the event, `render()` ran with `panelOpen = true` → `button.update(!!surface, /*allowFab*/ !panelOpen)` = `update(true, false)`. **The floating fallback is suppressed when the panel is open** (`calendar.content.ts:262`, `:505`). So if the *injected native* button hasn't landed yet, there is **no button at all**. - On a freshly‑opened popover, `readSurface()` returns null until the dialog is recognized as an event surface (`isEventDialog` needs a `data-eventid`, magic text, or ≥2 icon‑led rows — `calendar.content.ts:523‑528`). On first paint these can all be briefly absent, so the button doesn't inject until the 150ms retries / 1s safety poll catch up. "Sticky recognition" only helps *after* the first acceptance, so it doesn't cover the very first open. → button absent first time, present later. ## The flaws, ranked **A. Sync is primary‑calendar‑only (most likely your root cause).** `listEvents` hits `calendars/primary/events` and nothing else (`calendar.ts:183`), with the limitation documented at `calendar.ts:157‑171` and in your own memory note. If this event lives on a **secondary calendar you own** (or is otherwise not on your primary), sync structurally cannot see it → empty homescreen, no badge, and the nudge can only ever be the degraded title‑less optimistic one that expires in 12s. This is the single best explanation for "DOM saw it, sync didn't." **B. Homescreen and nudge use different data sources, so they contradict each other.** The nudge = sync ∪ optimistic; the homescreen = sync only. You can be looking at a visitor event, get nudged about it, and still see "no upcoming events." That inconsistency is itself a bug, independent of (A). **C. Optimistic title can't be read from the popover** (`snapshotOf`, the ``‑only scrape). Even on a primary‑calendar event there's a window where the nudge is nameless before sync backfills. **D. The empty state can't tell "not checked yet" from "checked, none found."** `useVisitorEvents` is `enabled: !eid && !!auth.data?.signedIn`; while auth is still loading the query is disabled, `isLoading` is false, so `EmptyState` immediately renders "No upcoming visitor events found" before it has actually looked (`EmptyState.tsx:72`, `:111`). Reads as a definitive "none" during a loading window. **E. `runSync` has a read‑modify‑write race on the shared sync token + marked map.** `LIST_VISITOR_EVENTS` calls `runSync` directly (`background.ts:297`) with no guard against the 1‑minute alarm's `doSync` running concurrently. Both read `markedEvents`/`syncToken`, both call `listEvents`, both write back — classic lost update: an event marked by one run can be clobbered by the other, and won't reappear because the token has advanced. Low‑frequency but a real correctness smell. Fix is a single in‑flight promise singleton around `runSync`. **F. Button suppression when the panel is open is a deliberate choice that backfires.** Functionally auto‑follow already loaded the event for you, but the "sometimes there's a Manage button, sometimes not" inconsistency is confusing. Worth either always injecting the native button (it's not redundant — it's the labeled affordance) or showing a quiet panel‑open hint. ## What I'd fix, and how Cheap, high‑confidence, no new scopes: - **C:** add a popover title fallback in `snapshotOf` (dialog `aria-label` → `[role="heading"]`/`h1`/`h2` → input). - **B/D:** make the homescreen agree with the nudge — seed `EmptyState` from `GET_NUDGE_TARGETS` (sync ∪ optimistic) and distinguish "checking…" from "none found." - **E:** wrap `runSync` in an in‑flight singleton so the alarm and the panel can't race. - **F:** stop suppressing the labeled button when the panel is open (or inject it regardless). Real work, needs a decision: - **A:** covering secondary calendars needs the `calendar.calendarlist.readonly` scope + per‑calendar sync tokens (enumerate `accessRole=owner` only). That's the deferred item finally biting. Before I touch code, one question that decides whether (A) is the actual root or just a contributor: **Was this event on your *primary/main* Google Calendar, or on a secondary calendar (a different colored calendar you own / a shared one)? And were you the organizer, or were you invited to it?** If you're not sure, open `chrome://extensions` → Auxilio → "service worker", reload the calendar tab, and tell me what the `[auxilio]` logs say (especially any "sync failed" / lapse). That'll confirm primary‑vs‑secondary vs a sync error. Want me to go ahead and implement the four cheap fixes (B–F) now while you check the calendar question?

content.text:
Good question — "secondary calendar" is a Google Calendar concept, not something specific to our code. Here's what it means and why it matters here.

## Primary vs. secondary calendars

In Google Calendar, every account has exactly **one primary calendar** — it's the one named after you (your email address, e.g. `junaidamdx@gmail.com`). You can't delete or rename it. It's the default place events land.

**Secondary calendars** are any *additional* calendars that show up in your left sidebar under "My calendars" / "Other calendars":
- A calendar you created yourself (e.g. "Office Visitors", "Team Events", "Bookings") — these live under your account but are **not** your primary.
- A calendar someone **shared with you** that you have write access to (e.g. a shared "Reception" or "Front Desk" calendar).
- A resource/room calendar, a team calendar, etc.

Each of these has its own calendar ID (something like `c_abc123...@group.calendar.google.com`), separate from your primary (`primary` / your email).

## Why it matters for the bug

Our background sync only ever asks Google about **one** calendar:

```ts
new URL('https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events')  // calendar.ts:183
```

`primary` literally means "the account's main calendar." So:

- If your visitor event was created **on your primary calendar** → sync should see it (and then a different cause is at play — likely a sync error or consistency lag).
- If it was created **on a secondary calendar** — say someone made an "Office Visitors" calendar and the event with the magic address lives there — then sync **never queries that calendar**, so the event is invisible to the homescreen list, the badge, and the real (titled) nudge. The only thing that still works is the in‑page DOM detection, because that reads whatever event popover you happen to open, regardless of which calendar it belongs to. That exactly matches what you saw.

## How to check which one it is

In Google Calendar's left sidebar, look at **"My calendars."** If you only have the one named after you, every event is on your primary — and the secondary‑calendar theory is ruled out (we'd look at sync errors instead). If there are extra calendars there, open the event and check: in the event detail/edit view, Google shows which calendar it belongs to (a colored dot + calendar name, usually near the title or in a "Calendar" dropdown on the edit page). If that calendar is **not** your own name, it's a secondary calendar — and that's the bug.

Can you tell me what you see there? That single fact decides whether the fix is "just the cheap consistency + title fixes" or "we also need to add the secondary‑calendar scope (`calendar.calendarlist.readonly`) and sync those calendars too."

```
[auxilio] sync done { mode, magicAddress, itemsReturned, markedTotal, newMarked }
```
…and if an event's payload contains the magic address but `isMarked()` didn't flag it, a loud:
```
[auxilio] sync MISS: magic address in payload but isMarked() is false { id, summary, location, attendees, status }
```
This is the piece that tells us *why* a primary‑calendar event is missed. The three possible outcomes map cleanly to the three candidate causes:

- **You see the `sync MISS` warning** → the event came back from the API but `isMarked` rejected it. Almost certainly `events.list` returned the event with a **trimmed/empty `attendees` list** (a known Google quirk where the list endpoint omits attendees that `events.get` includes), so the magic address isn't matched. - **`itemsReturned` is large but no MISS, and your event isn't in it** → it's a **sync‑token/consistency** problem (the incremental delta didn't carry it). Fix: force a periodic full resync, or stop freezing `timeMin`.
- **No `sync done` line at all, or a `sync failed` / `refresh failed` warning** → the Calendar API call is **throwing** (e.g. scope/token). Fix is on the auth/token side.

**3. Fixed the sync race** (`calendar-sync.ts`). `runSync` is now coalesced to one in‑flight call, so the 1‑minute alarm and the panel's on‑demand sync can't clobber each other's writes to the shared sync token / marked map.

## Opening the service‑worker console

1. In Chrome, go to `chrome://extensions` (type it in the address bar).

content.text:
The log is the smoking gun. `mode: "incremental"`, `itemsReturned: 0` — Google returned **zero changes** for your stored sync token, and `markedTotal: 0` means the event is **not** in the cached set. So the event isn't being missed *right now* by `isMarked`; it was missed earlier and the token has since advanced past it.

## What's actually happening (root cause)

Google's **incremental** sync only ever returns events that **changed since the token was issued**. Each successful sync advances (consumes) the token. So this sequence permanently hides an event:

1. The event's create/modify delta was consumed by some earlier sync.
2. At that moment it was **not** marked — most likely because an earlier build used a *different* magic address (your `.env` is `findajx@gmail.com`, the example default is `visitors@auxilio.app`), so `isMarked` returned false then.
3. The token advanced past that delta. Now incremental sync returns `0 items` forever, so the event is **never re-evaluated** — even though the address now matches.

That's why `events.get` works (panel loads it) but the list/badge/synced-nudge are blank: nothing re-scans an event once its delta is consumed. The current code only ever does a full re-scan on a `410` token expiry — which may not happen for a long time.

## The fix

Two parts: (1) force a full re-scan on every install/reload and periodically, so the set self-heals; (2) make sure a full re-scan doesn't fire a notification for every existing event. Let me read the current function and rewrite it cleanly.

content.text:
Built clean as **0.6.1**. Here's the full picture.

## Root cause (confirmed by your log)

`mode: "incremental"`, `itemsReturned: 0`, `markedTotal: 0` told the whole story:

- Google's **incremental** sync only returns events that changed **since the stored token**, and each successful sync **consumes/advances** that token.
- Your event's create/modify delta was consumed by an earlier sync — almost certainly back when an earlier build used a **different magic address** (`.env` = `findajx@gmail.com` vs the example default `visitors@auxilio.app`), so `isMarked` returned false at that moment.
- The token advanced past it. From then on, incremental sync returns `0 items` and the event is **never re-evaluated**, even though the address now matches. `events.get` still works, which is why the panel loads it and only the optimistic DOM nudge fires.

The old code only ever did a full re-scan on a `410` token expiry, which can take a very long time — so the miss was effectively permanent.

## The fix (v0.6.1)

**Self-healing full re-scan** (`calendar-sync.ts`, `background.ts`):
- On every **install/reload/update**, the sync token is cleared (`clearSyncToken` in `onInstalled`) → next sync re-scans the whole forward window. This is what makes a config change like the magic address actually take effect.
- **Periodically** (every 12h) the token is dropped and the window re-scanned, so production drift self-heals without a reload.
- A full re-scan **rebuilds** the marked set from scratch (stale events drop out) but computes "genuinely new" against the *previous* set — so it does **not** fire a notification storm for events you already knew about.

Plus the earlier fixes still in this build:
- **Nameless nudge** → popover title now read from the dialog heading.
- **Sync race** → `runSync` coalesced to one in-flight call.
- **List won't blank** on a transient sync error (serves cached set).
- **Button affordance** kept when the panel is open.

## What to do now

1. `chrome://extensions` → **reload** the Auxilio card → confirm it shows **0.6.1**. (The reload itself triggers the full re-scan.)
2. Open the service-worker console and reload your Calendar tab.
3. You should now see something like:
   ```
   [auxilio] sync done { mode: "full", itemsReturned: <N>, markedTotal: 1, newMarked: 0 }
   ```
   and the event should appear in the homescreen list + the toolbar badge.

**Two outcomes to report back:**
- **`markedTotal: 1` (or more)** → fixed; the event shows up. 
- **A `[auxilio] sync MISS …` warning appears** → then it's the *other* candidate (Google's `events.list` is returning the event with a trimmed `attendees` list), and I'll add an `events.get` fallback for candidate events. The MISS log will show exactly what attendees came back.

If you confirm it's working after the reload, I'll strip the verbose `sync done`/`sync MISS` diagnostics back down to a quieter level (or leave them behind a debug flag — your call).

2

4	**Scope:** Why the calendar surfaces sometimes show the wrong pass status —
5	incorrect "passes sent", false "already sent", and failure to detect an
6	invitation already linked to an event. Covers the **Chrome extension**
7	(`auxilio-extension`), the **Workspace add-on** (`auxilio-workspace`), the
8	**Slack** invite path, and the **ops-dashboard** single + batch invite flows.
9	**Type:** Analysis / audit. **F2 + the contained half of F1 implemented 2026-06-22**
10	(see §8). The recurring-occurrence half of F1 remains deferred.
11	
12	---
13

15

17	`POST /api/visitor/calendar/addon/draft` → `getOrCreateDraft()`
18	(`office-ops-engine/src/mastra/modules/visitor/visit-drafts.ts`).
19	
20	The status a host sees is purely a render of two engine-computed fields:
21	
22	- `draft.roster[].status` — `pending | sent | cancelled`
23	- `draft.materialized` — drives "Manage / Review & update" vs "Send"
24

26

# Calendar Visitor — Pass-Status Correctness Audit

**Date:** 2026-06-22
**Scope:** Why the calendar surfaces sometimes show the wrong pass status —
incorrect "passes sent", false "already sent", and failure to detect an
invitation already linked to an event. Covers the **Chrome extension**
(`auxilio-extension`), the **Workspace add-on** (`auxilio-workspace`), the
**Slack** invite path, and the **ops-dashboard** single + batch invite flows.
**Type:** Analysis / audit. **F2 + the contained half of F1 implemented 2026-06-22**
(see §8). The recurring-occurrence half of F1 remains deferred.

---

## 1. How status actually flows (the single source of truth)

Every calendar surface funnels into one engine endpoint:
`POST /api/visitor/calendar/addon/draft` → `getOrCreateDraft()`
(`office-ops-engine/src/mastra/modules/visitor/visit-drafts.ts`).

The status a host sees is purely a render of two engine-computed fields:

- `draft.roster[].status` — `pending | sent | cancelled`
- `draft.materialized` — drives "Manage / Review & update" vs "Send"

Clients only render what the engine returns:

- Add-on: `Code.js` → `statusLabel_` (`:316`), `buildStatLine_` (`:330`).
- Extension: `features/visit/RosterRow.tsx` (`:35-58`), `features/visit/VisitPanel.tsx`
  (`sentCount` `:179`, `materialized` gating `:206-207`).

**Implication:** the wrongness is introduced server-side, in two functions —
`adoptExistingPasses()` and the "self-heal" block of `getOrCreateDraft()`. The
clients are faithful. Most fixes are server-side and fix all surfaces at once.

Detection of "is there already an invitation linked to this event?" depends
entirely on the `calendar_event_links` ledger being keyed on a **stable,
identical `iCalUID` across every surface and every open**. That assumption is
where most of the breakage lives.

---

## 2. Findings (prioritised)

| #  | Issue | Severity | Primary location |
|----|-------|----------|------------------|
| F1 | Link detection is a single exact-match on the literal `iCalUid` — collides for recurring series, misses with no fallback | **High** | `calendar-ingest.ts:242`, `visit-drafts.ts:293` |
| F2 | Adoption is skipped for default-off (internal) guests even when a link-verified pass exists | **High** | `visit-drafts.ts:312` |
| F8 | Cannot cancel the last/only pass from either calendar surface; `cancel-guest` endpoint unused; extension dead-tap | **Medium** | `Code.js:689`, `VisitPanel.tsx`, `routes.ts:810` |
| F9 | No dedup at the create chokepoint — dashboard single/batch + Slack issue duplicates on retry | **Medium** | `routes.ts:4401/4573`, `slack-handler.ts:515` |
| F3 | Cross-channel passes (dashboard/Slack-non-write) are invisible to the calendar surface by design → unseen duplicates | **Medium** | `visit-drafts.ts:312-317` |
| F5 | `materialized` flips to "Manage" on a cancelled-only roster (0 active passes) | **Low** | `visit-drafts.ts:511` |
| F6 | Legacy mis-linked external `sent` rows survive self-heal (false "Pass sent") | **Low** | `visit-drafts.ts:485-486` |
| F4 | Self-heal conflates "honourable now" with "was issued" (`rejected` reset, time-expiry ignored) | **Low** | `visit-drafts.ts:485`, `db.ts:313` |
| F7 | Two-key divergence can strand a stale `sent` | **Low** | (same root as F1) |
| F10 | Recurring instance-id vs base-id mismatch defeats the recovery bridge | **Low** | `Code.js:loadEvent_`, `calendar.ts:fetchActiveEvent` |

The two **High** items (F1, F2) account for the reported symptoms and are both
fixable server-side in `visit-drafts.ts` + `calendar-ingest.ts`.

---

# Calendar Extension — Visitor Event Sync Visibility Bug

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Scope:** Why a real visitor event (magic address present, on the host's
**primary** calendar) failed to appear in the Chrome extension's homescreen list,
toolbar badge, and titled nudge — while the in-page DOM nudge still fired (with no
event name). Covers `auxilio-extension` only (client-side sync). The pass-status
server-side issues are a separate audit
(`2026-06-22_calendar_visitor_pass_status_bugs_analysis.md`).
**Type:** Bug analysis + fix. **Implemented 2026-06-23 in extension v0.6.1.**

---

## 1. Reported symptoms

A host had an event with the magic address (`findajx@gmail.com`), 4 attendees
(host + magic address + 2 guests), on their **primary** calendar. In the
extension:

1. **Homescreen** showed "No upcoming visitor events found" even though the event
   existed and was open.
2. After viewing the event and closing the panel, the **in-page nudge** appeared
   but with **no event name** — just "Visitor event needs passes." + Manage.
3. The injected **"Manage Visitors" button** was missing on the first open.

---

## 2. How the surfaces are fed (the relevant data paths)

- **Homescreen list / badge / titled nudge** ← background **sync cache**:
  `useVisitorEvents` → `LIST_VISITOR_EVENTS` → `runSync()` + `listMarked()`
  (`hooks.ts:179`, `background.ts:295`, `lib/calendar-sync.ts`). Sync-derived
  only; **no optimistic fallback**.
- **In-page nudge** ← background sync **∪ an optimistic DOM guess** captured by
  the content script when you view an event whose visible text contains the magic
  address (`calendar.content.ts:239-255`, `mountNudge`/`addOptimistic`).

Key consequence: the nudge can show an event the homescreen does not, because the
nudge has the optimistic path and the homescreen does not.

---

## 3. Root cause — incremental sync never re-evaluates a once-missed event

Diagnostic log captured at repro time:

```
[auxilio] sync done { mode: "incremental", magicAddress: "findajx@gmail.com",
                      itemsReturned: 0, markedTotal: 0, newMarked: 0 }
```

Google Calendar's **incremental** `events.list` (with a `syncToken`) returns only
events that **changed since the token was issued**, and every successful sync
**advances/consumes** the token (`lib/calendar.ts:173-216`). That produces a
permanent blind spot:

1. The event's create/modify delta was consumed by an earlier sync.
2. At that moment `isMarked()` returned **false** — most likely because an earlier
   build used a **different magic address** (`.env` `findajx@gmail.com` vs the
   `.env.example` default `visitors@auxilio.app`), so the address did not match.
3. The token advanced past the delta. From then on incremental sync returns
   `0 items`, so the event is **never re-examined** — even after the address
   config matched again.

`events.get` still resolves the event (so the side panel loads it fine), which is
why only the optimistic DOM nudge fired. The old code only did a full re-scan on a
`410` token expiry (`calendar-sync.ts`), which can be a very long time away — so
the miss was effectively permanent.

**Proven, not guessed:** the optimistic nudge firing means the build's
`MAGIC_ADDRESS` *did* match the event text (so it is not a current mismatch), and
`itemsReturned: 0` / `markedTotal: 0` means the event was simply never in the
synced set — a stale-token miss, not a live `isMarked` failure.

### Secondary defects found in the same flow

- **Nameless nudge:** `snapshotOf` read the title only from an `<input>`, which
  exists on the full editor but **not** the detail popover (the common case), so
  the optimistic nudge had an empty title (`calendar.content.ts` `snapshotOf`).
- **Sync race:** `LIST_VISITOR_EVENTS` called `runSync` with no guard against the
  1-minute alarm's concurrent `doSync` — both read the same baseline and the later
  write clobbered the earlier (lost update on the shared token / marked map).
- **List blanks on a transient error:** a refresh-sync throw made the
  `LIST_VISITOR_EVENTS` query fail, rendering as "no events" rather than serving
  the cached set (`background.ts`).
- **Missing button when panel open:** the floating fallback was suppressed while
  the panel was open, so if the native injection lost the first-open race there
  was **no** button at all (`calendar.content.ts` `render` / `createButtonUI`).

---

## 4. Fix (extension v0.6.1)

**Self-healing full re-scan** (`calendar-sync.ts`, `background.ts`) — the core fix:

- `clearSyncToken()` drops the token + last-full stamp; called from
  `chrome.runtime.onInstalled` so **every install/reload/update** forces a full
  tokenless re-scan. This is what makes a config change (e.g. the magic address)
  actually take effect.
- **Periodic** full re-scan every `FULL_RESYNC_INTERVAL_MS` (12h): if the token is
  older than the interval it is dropped before the sync, so production drift
  self-heals without a reload.
- A full re-scan **rebuilds** the marked set from scratch (stale events drop out)
  but computes "genuinely new" against the **previous** set (`prevMarked`), so it
  does **not** fire a notification storm for events already known.

Bundled secondary fixes:

- Popover title fallback in `snapshotOf` (dialog heading / `aria-label`) → nudge
  shows the event name even before sync reconciles.
- `runSync` coalesced to one in-flight promise → no concurrent-sync lost update.
- `LIST_VISITOR_EVENTS` serves the cached set if the refresh sync throws.
- Floating button fallback kept available when the panel is open.
- Diagnostics: `[auxilio] sync done {…}` per sync, and a `[auxilio] sync MISS …`
  warning when the magic address is in a payload but `isMarked()` is false.

---

## 5. Open item — confirm there is no `events.list` attendee trim

After v0.6.1 forces a full re-scan, two outcomes:

- `markedTotal ≥ 1`, event appears → **fixed** (stale-token miss; this is the
  expected outcome).
- A `[auxilio] sync MISS …` warning appears → Google's `events.list` is returning
  the event with a **trimmed `attendees`** list (a second, distinct cause). Fix
  would be an `events.get` fallback for candidate events to read authoritative
  attendees before deciding `isMarked`.

Once confirmed working, the verbose `sync done` / `sync MISS` logs can be dropped
to a quieter level or gated behind a debug flag.

---

## 6. Related

- Nudge reliability hardening (handled-overlay, auth-lapse banner, recurring
  de-dup): memory `project_extension_nudge_reliability`.
- Secondary-calendar coverage remains **deferred** (primary-only sync,
  `calendar.ts:157-171`); not the cause here (event was on primary).
</content>
</invoke>

Done. Created `Planning-docs/2026-06-23_extension_visitor_event_sync_visibility_bug.md`.

I put it in a **new** dated doc rather than the existing `2026-06-22_calendar_visitor_pass_status_bugs_analysis.md`, because that one is scoped to *server-side pass-status correctness* (iCalUID linking, adoption) — a different layer from this *client-side sync-visibility* bug. Mixing them would blur its scope.

The doc captures, in the house style (Date / Scope / Type header, file:line refs):

- **Symptoms** — the three things you saw.
- **Data paths** — why the homescreen (sync-only) and the nudge (sync ∪ optimistic) can disagree.
- **Root cause** — incremental sync consumes its token and never re-evaluates a once-missed event; the diagnostic log as evidence; and the proof it wasn't a current address mismatch.
- **Secondary defects** — nameless nudge, sync race, list-blanks-on-error, missing button.
- **The v0.6.1 fix** — self-healing full re-scan (on install + 12h periodic, storm-safe), plus the bundled fixes.
- **Open item** — the `events.list` attendee-trim possibility to confirm via the `sync MISS` log, and the note to quiet the diagnostics once verified.

It also cross-links the pass-status audit, the `project_extension_nudge_reliability` memory, and the deferred secondary-calendar limitation.

One thing still pending from the last step: after you reload to 0.6.1, the `sync done` line should show `mode: "full"` with the event marked. If you paste that back, I'll mark the open item resolved in the doc (and we can decide whether to quiet the diagnostics).
